name: Milk-Fed Lamb with Garlic and Tarragon
recipeIngredient: Suckling lamb leg, about 1 kg (approximately 1.5 kg if using regular lamb)
Fresh tarragon leaves (or dried tarragon, 1 tablespoon)
Jellied veal or chicken stock, 1 to 2 tablespoons
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 190C.
2. If using a glass roaster, brush it with olive oil. Place the lamb leg in the roaster and brown all over.
3. If using foil, place the lamb leg on a sheet of foil and brown it in a pan brushed with olive oil.
4. Cut 4 cloves of garlic into slivers.
5. Make slits all over the lamb and tuck the garlic slivers inside.
6. Dust the lamb generously with salt and pepper.
7. Add fresh tarragon leaves or dried tarragon, and jellied stock to the leg in the roaster or on foil.
8. Securely seal the seams of foil if wrapping the leg.
9. Roast for 35 minutes (for suckling lamb wrapped in foil, still pink inside) to 1 hour (for larger leg in roaster, well done).
10. Let rest for 10 minutes before carving.
11. Serve with juices spooned over the meat. Good with creamy mashed potatoes to absorb the juices.
